concept.text: Dogs go woof! Cats go meow! Cows go moo! Animals make different sounds.

questions.prompt: What sound does a cow make?

concept.text: A rainbow has red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple! It shows up after rain.
concept.funFact: Rainbows are made of sunlight and water!

concept.text: We have 2 eyes to see, 2 ears to hear, 1 nose to smell, and 1 mouth to talk and eat!
concept.funFact: Your ears help you keep your balance too!

questions.prompt: How many eyes do you have?

concept.text: In the day the sun is up and it's bright. At night the moon is out and it's dark!
concept.funFact: We sleep at night so our bodies can rest!

questions.prompt: What is in the sky during the day?
Do we usually sleep at night?
Is it bright or dark at night?

concept.text: Wash your hands! Eat fruits and veggies! Drink water! These help you grow strong.
concept.funFact: Apples and carrots help keep you healthy!

concept.text: Our solar system has 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune. The Sun is a star at the center, and they all orbit around it.
concept.funFact: Jupiter is so big that all other planets could fit inside it!

questions.prompt: How many planets are in our solar system?
The closest planet to the Sun is ____.
Earth is the ___ planet from the sun.

concept.text: Matter exists in three main states: solid (like ice), liquid (like water), and gas (like steam). Heating ice melts it into water; heating water turns it to steam!
concept.funFact: Water is the only substance found naturally in all three states on Earth!

questions.prompt: Heating a solid usually makes it...

concept.text: Your body has systems: the heart pumps blood (circulatory), lungs help you breathe (respiratory), and bones give you shape (skeletal). They all work together!
concept.funFact: An adult human has 206 bones — but babies are born with around 270!

questions.prompt: How many bones in an adult body?

concept.text: Mammals (like dogs) have fur and feed milk to babies. Reptiles (snakes, lizards) are cold-blooded. Amphibians (frogs) live in water and on land. Birds have feathers and lay eggs.
concept.funFact: A hummingbird's heart beats over 1,200 times per minute!

concept.text: Cross at crosswalks, look both ways. Wear a helmet on a bike. Know the emergency number (911 in USA). Never share personal info with strangers online.
concept.funFact: Bike helmets reduce the risk of serious head injury by about 70%!

questions.prompt: What do you wear riding a bike?

concept.text: Everything is made of atoms. The Periodic Table organizes elements (like Hydrogen, Oxygen, Carbon, Gold). Each has a symbol and atomic number based on its protons.
concept.funFact: Gold's symbol is Au, from the Latin word 'aurum' meaning 'shining dawn'.

questions.prompt: Atomic number is based on the number of protons.

concept.text: An ecosystem is a community of living things and their environment. Food chains show energy flow: sun → plants → herbivores → carnivores. Decomposers (fungi, bacteria) recycle nutrients.
concept.funFact: Coral reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean but house 25% of marine species!

questions.prompt: What's at the start of most food chains?
Animals that eat only plants are called ____.
A coral reef is an example of a...

concept.text: Isaac Newton discovered gravity. Marie Curie pioneered research on radioactivity (and won 2 Nobel Prizes). Albert Einstein revolutionized physics with relativity. Charles Darwin explained evolution.
concept.funFact: Marie Curie is the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences.

questions.prompt: Theory of evolution was proposed by...
Einstein is famous for the theory of relativity.

concept.text: Energy comes in many forms: kinetic (motion), potential (stored), thermal (heat), electrical, chemical, and nuclear. Energy can change form but is never created or destroyed.
concept.funFact: The 'Law of Conservation of Energy' says energy can only be transformed — not lost!

questions.prompt: Energy can be created from nothing.

concept.text: Democracy lets citizens vote for leaders. The US has three branches: Executive (President), Legislative (Congress), Judicial (Supreme Court). Each checks the others.
concept.funFact: The US Constitution was signed in 1787 — over 230 years ago!

questions.prompt: How many branches in US government?
The President is in the ____ branch.
In a democracy, citizens vote for leaders.
